mission.title: Install and Configure Oxia on Kubernetes
mission.description: Oxia is a robust and scalable metadata store and coordination system designed for large-scale distributed systems. Installing Oxia allows you to efficiently manage metadata in cloud-native applications, ensuring scalability and high availability.

steps.title: Add the Oxia Helm repository
steps.description:
Add the official Oxia Helm chart repository to your Helm configuration.
```bash
helm repo add oxia https://oxia-db.github.io/charts
helm repo update
```

steps.title: Install Oxia using Helm
steps.description:
Install Oxia in a new namespace called 'oxia' with the specified version.
```bash
helm install oxia oxia/oxia --version v0.15.3 --namespace oxia --create-namespace
```

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
Check if the Oxia pods are running successfully in the 'oxia' namespace.
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace oxia
```

steps.title: Configure resource limits
steps.description:
Update the Oxia deployment to set resource limits for better performance.
```bash
kubectl set resources deployment/oxia --namespace oxia --limits=cpu=500m,memory=512Mi --requests=cpu=250m,memory=256Mi
```

steps.title: Set up port forwarding for access
steps.description:
Forward a local port to the Oxia service to access it.
```bash
kubectl port-forward svc/oxia --namespace oxia 8080:80
```

resolution.summary: A successful installation of Oxia will show the Oxia pods running in the 'oxia' namespace. You will also be able to access the service via port 8080 on your local machine.
codeSnippets:
helm repo add oxia https://oxia-db.github.io/charts
helm repo update
helm install oxia oxia/oxia --version v0.15.3 --namespace oxia --create-namespace

uninstall.title: Remove the Helm release
uninstall.description:
Uninstall the Oxia release from the cluster using Helm. This will remove the deployment and associated resources managed by Helm.
```bash
helm uninstall oxia --namespace oxia
```

uninstall.title: Clean up CRDs and persistent resources
uninstall.description:
Remove any Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs) and persistent volume claims (PVCs) that were created during the installation. Ensure that all resources are cleaned up to avoid orphaned resources.
```bash
kubectl delete crd $(kubectl get crd | grep oxia | awk '{print $1}')
kubectl delete pvc --all --namespace oxia
```

uninstall.title: Remove namespace and verify
uninstall.description:
Delete the 'oxia' namespace to remove all remaining resources and verify that it has been removed successfully.
```bash
kubectl delete namespace oxia
kubectl get namespaces | grep oxia
```

upgrade.title: Backup current state
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, back up the current state of the Oxia deployment. This can include exporting configurations or taking snapshots of persistent volumes if necessary.
```bash
kubectl get all --namespace oxia -o yaml > oxia-backup.yaml
```

upgrade.title: Update repo and run upgrade command
upgrade.description:
Update the Helm repository and perform the upgrade to the latest version of Oxia. Replace 'v0.15.3' with the desired version if necessary.
```bash
helm repo update
helm upgrade oxia oxia/oxia --version v0.15.4 --namespace oxia
```

upgrade.title: Verify the upgrade
upgrade.description:
Check the status of the Oxia pods to ensure they are running the upgraded version without issues.
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace oxia
```

troubleshooting.title: Pods stuck in CrashLoopBackOff
troubleshooting.description:
If the Oxia pods are in a CrashLoopBackOff state, check the logs to diagnose the issue. This may indicate a misconfiguration or resource limits being exceeded.
```bash
kubectl logs <pod-name> --namespace oxia
```

troubleshooting.title: Service not reachable
troubleshooting.description:
If you cannot access the Oxia service, ensure that the port forwarding is set up correctly and that the service is running. Check the service status and logs.
```bash
kubectl get svc --namespace oxia
kubectl logs <pod-name> --namespace oxia
```

troubleshooting.title: Insufficient resources
troubleshooting.description:
If the deployment fails due to insufficient resources, check the resource requests and limits set for the Oxia deployment and adjust them accordingly.
```bash
kubectl describe deployment oxia --namespace oxia
```

troubleshooting.title: Helm release not found
troubleshooting.description:
If you encounter an error stating that the Helm release is not found, verify that the release name and namespace are correct. List all releases in the namespace to confirm.
```bash
helm list --namespace oxia
```

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running and Helm installed on your local machine.
